reasoning: Daily mean temperatures in Lyon steadily decrease from late October to late January. Normal climate averages suggest late October is around 11.5°C. For Nov 2, expected mean is ~10°C, giving a difference diff=~-1.5°C. For Nov 25, expected mean represents late autumn at ~6.5°C (diff=~-5.0°C). For Jan 24, typically the coldest part of the year, mean is ~3.5°C (diff=~-8.0°C). Assuming a standard deviation for the difference in daily temperatures between two independently drawn autumn/winter days is ~7°C, we can model the probabilities using a normal distribution. Evaluating P(Z > Expected_Drop/StdDev), we get roughly P1 ~ 42%, P2 ~ 24%, and P3 ~ 14%. While longer time horizons naturally increase forecasting uncertainty (which would classically push probabilities towards 0.5), the deterministic seasonal cooling strongly lowers the base rate of observing a warmer day than late October down to ~14% by late January.
